question: White background canvas, vector information dashboard, using 12-column grid alignment (uniform column width, uniform column spacing), 5% margin on all sides. Overall layout from top to bottom in three rows: top title bar approximately 12% height, middle main body approximately 78%, bottom indicator area approximately 10%. Top title bar: left side has a solid square LOGO at 70% of the title bar height, center has large text DASHBOARD, right side has two lines of small text DATE 01 and VER 1. Middle main body divided into three columns of equal width and equal height: left column is a table (5 columns × 8 rows, first row merged as header H, first column as row labels R1-R7, thin black table lines, alternating light gray background for odd/even rows); middle column upper half is a grouped bar chart (4 groups G1-G4, each group has 3 bars A/B/C, uniform bar width, uniform group spacing, with coordinate axes and tick marks, left Y-axis 0-100 in 5 divisions), middle column lower half is a line chart (12 points M1-M12, circle markers, with 4 light gray horizontal grid lines); right column upper half is a pie chart (5 sectors S1-S5, angles are 40°/60°/80°/100°/80° respectively, right side has vertical legend with 5 rows), right column lower half is a flowchart (START→P1→diamond D1, D1 branches to P2 and P3, both branches merge at P4→END; all connecting lines must be horizontal or vertical only, diagonal lines prohibited). Bottom indicator area: 4 equal-width KPI cards arranged horizontally, each card has a large number on top (01/02/03/04) and a small label below (K1-K4). All modules are outlined with thin rectangular border frames, color palette limited to black/gray/blue only, all text uses only Latin letters and numerals.

White background canvas, draw a three-swimlane flowchart (swimlane titles from top to bottom: USER, SERVICE, DB), each swimlane is an equal-height horizontal rectangular frame with a narrow title bar on the left side. Total flow nodes ≥16, node shape specifications: start/end nodes are capsule-shaped (stadium), tasks are rounded rectangles, decisions are diamonds, parallel fork/merge are represented by two thick vertical bars. Flow begins from START in the upper-left of the USER swimlane: START→F1→F2, cross-lane arrow to SERVICE swimlane S1→S2→decision G1. G1 No branch: to S3→cross back to USER to E1→decision G2, G2 Yes loops back to F1 forming a cycle, G2 No goes to END. G1 Yes branch: S4→cross to DB swimlane D1→D2→cross back to SERVICE to S5. Then parallel gateway G3 forks into two parallel tasks P1 and P2 (arranged vertically within the same swimlane), both merge at G4 then cross back to USER to OK→END. Requirements: all connecting lines must be orthogonal (horizontal/vertical only), cross-lane connectors must cross swimlane boundaries vertically; each node contains only its label (START, F1...) with text center-aligned; use only black and gray colors throughout, consistent line weight.

White background vector image. Left 65% area contains a four-layer organizational structure chart; right 35% area contains a relationship matrix table plus legend. Organizational structure chart: Layer 1 has 1 node C0 centered; Layer 2 has 3 nodes A1, A2, A3 arranged equidistantly on the same horizontal line, connected downward from C0; Layer 3 has 6 nodes B1-B6 (B1, B2 under A1; B3, B4 under A2; B5, B6 under A3); Layer 4 has 12 leaf nodes D1-D12 (2 leaf nodes below each B node). All nodes are identically sized rounded rectangles, with consistent vertical spacing between layers; connection lines are right-angle polylines with same-layer nodes aligned on a horizontal baseline. Additional dashed relationships: a dashed arrow points from B2 to B4; the connection line between A1 and D1 uses a dash-dot line. Right-side matrix table: 6 rows × 5 columns; top-left cell has a diagonal split header (ROW written in bottom-left, COL written in top-right); first row columns 2-5 have headers C1-C4; first column rows 2-6 have headers R1-R5. Data area (5×4) filled with symbols as follows: R1 row: ● ○ — ○; R2 row: ○ — ○ ●; R3 row: — ○ ● ○; R4 row: ○ ● ○ —; R5 row: ● ○ — ○. Below the matrix, place a three-row legend: ● L1, ○ L2, — L3. Entire image uses only black and gray colors; no perspective or shadows allowed; all text consists only of Latin letters, numbers, and symbols.

White background vertical canvas (aspect ratio approximately 4:5), divided into left and right columns: left column 60% featuring a radial mind map, right column 40% featuring a vertical timeline and small table. Left column mind map: a large central circle labeled T0, with 6 main branches radiating uniformly from the center in directions: straight up, upper-right, lower-right, straight down, lower-left, and upper-left; each main branch connects to a rounded rectangle node labeled A-F (the 6 nodes form a regular hexagonal layout). From each node A-F, 3 secondary branches extend, connecting to 3 small dots (no text); each small dot further branches into 2 even smaller leaf dots (no text). All connecting lines are smooth curves with uniform thickness, nodes evenly spaced, the entire structure symmetrical both horizontally and vertically. Right column timeline: a central vertical main line, with 7 milestone dots labeled T1-T7 placed equidistantly from top to bottom; each dot has a short horizontal line extending to the right connecting to a small rectangular label box labeled P1-P7. Between T3 and T4, a fork occurs: a branch line diverges from the main line to the right to an additional dot labeled X1, then returns to the main line connecting to T4, forming a bypass. At the bottom of the right column, a small 2-row by 3-column table with six cells in a checkerboard pattern fill (top-left cell is black). The entire image uses only black, gray, and blue colors.

White square canvas with a complex data table drawn in the center (approximately 85% of canvas width, 70% of canvas height), table lines are thin black lines, cells have ample white space. The table has 9 rows × 7 columns: Row 1 is the group header row, except for the top-left corner cell, Row 1 columns 2-4 are merged into G1, Row 1 columns 5-7 are merged into G2; the top-left corner cell has a diagonal header (ROW written in bottom-left, COL written in top-right). Row 2 is the sub-header row: Row 2 columns 2-7 display C1-C6 respectively; Row 2 column 1 is left blank. Rows 3-9 are data rows: column 1 displays R1-R7. Merged cell requirements: In R3 row, C2 and C3 are merged; in R5 row, C4 through C6 are merged; in R7 row, C1 and C2 are merged (where C1/C2 correspond to the C1/C2 data columns from Row 2). Inside each data cell (including merged large cells), draw a mini horizontal bar that fills from left to right, with length at only 5 levels: 20%/40%/60%/80%/100%, following a clear pattern per row: R1 row from C1 to C6 is 20/40/60/80/100/60; R2 row is 100/80/60/40/20/40; R3 row is 40/(merged cell=80)/60/40/20; R4 row is 60/60/60/60/60/60; R5 row is 20/40/60/(merged cell=100); R6 row is 80/60/40/20/40/60; R7 row is (merged cell=100)/80/60/40/20. Below the table, centered, place a small bar chart legend: 5 small bars increasing from 20% to 100% labeled 20 40 60 80 100. Black and gray colors only, sans-serif font, all text uses only Latin letters and numbers.

White background horizontal canvas, depicting a strictly grid-aligned multi-panel infographic. The upper half occupies 55% of the height, the lower half occupies 45%. The upper half is divided into left and right sections: the left side contains a 3-row × 2-column matrix of small multiple bar charts (6 small charts total, each equal in width and height, with consistent spacing), and the right side contains 1 large line chart. Each small bar chart: has fine gray coordinate axes with 3 vertical bars (labeled A/B/C), bars have consistent width; the bar height patterns for the 6 small charts are as follows: Chart 1 (20,40,60), Chart 2 (60,40,20), Chart 3 (30,80,50), Chart 4 (50,30,80), Chart 5 (70,70,20), Chart 6 (20,70,70) (expressed as percentage heights, bars rising from the same baseline). The right-side large line chart: contains 3 lines L1/L2/L3 within the same coordinate system, each with 8 points marked by hollow circles; L1 trends upward (10→80), L2 trends downward (80→10), L3 rises then falls (20→70→50), with 4 light gray horizontal grid lines. The lower half: the left side contains a ring chart occupying 55% of the lower half's width, with 5 sectors at angles of 90°/45°/135°/60°/30° respectively, each sector has a leader line extending outward connecting to labels S1-S5 outside the ring, labels evenly distributed around the circumference; the right side contains a 4-row × 3-column small table, the first row contains headers H1-H3, the first column contains R1-R3, the remaining cells are filled with alternating ● and ○ symbols (starting with ● from the top-left data cell). All module boundaries are marked with thin rectangular frames, the entire design uses only black/gray/blue colors, all text uses only Latin letters and numbers.

White background horizontal canvas. The upper 70% contains a fishbone diagram (cause-and-effect diagram), and the lower 30% is divided into left and right sections: the left side is a checklist table, and the right side is a bar chart. Fishbone diagram: a main spine extends horizontally from left to right, with a large rectangular effect box labeled "EFFECT" connected at the right end; 6 main bones are connected to the main spine at equal intervals, each at approximately 60° angle to the spine, alternating in direction up and down (1st bone upward, 2nd bone downward, and so on), the 6 main bones are labeled A-F respectively. Each main bone has 2 shorter sub-bones connected to it (tilted in the same direction), with small dots at the ends of the sub-bones, no text. All bone lines are uniform in thickness, maintaining symmetry and even spacing. Lower left checklist table: 6 rows × 4 columns, first row headers H1-H4, first column row titles R1-R5; each data cell contains a hollow square checkbox, checked in a diagonal pattern: R1H1, R2H2, R3H3, R4H4, R5H1 (the rest empty). Lower right bar chart: 8 vertical bars side by side, uniform bar width and spacing, with coordinate axes and scale marks; bar heights are 10/30/50/70/90/70/50/30 (relative heights) respectively, labeled B1-B8 below the bars. Only black/gray/blue colors, no gradients or shadows, all text uses only Latin letters and numbers.

White background horizontal canvas (aspect ratio approximately 3:2), depicting a layered data pipeline/system architecture diagram, using a strict 5-column grid alignment throughout. From left to right, 5 stage containers, each stage being a large rectangle with a light gray border and a title: L1, L2, L3, L4, L5. The five containers are equal in width and height and arranged horizontally with equal spacing. Inside each container, place several small modules (rounded rectangles) connected by arrows: L1 contains 3 source modules S1-S3 arranged vertically; L2 contains 2 ingestion modules I1-I2 arranged vertically; L3 contains 4 processing modules P1-P4 forming a 2×2 grid; L4 contains 3 storage modules D1-D3 arranged horizontally; L5 contains 2 output modules O1-O2 arranged vertically. Connection rules: from each S module in L1, connect separately to both I1 and I2 in L2 (forming a fan-out); from I1 and I2 in L2, connect to P1 and P2 in L3 (connecting only to the upper row of processing modules); P1 and P2 then connect to P3 and P4 respectively (vertical arrows between the upper and lower rows); P3 and P4 in L3 converge to D2 in L4 (two arrows pointing to the same module); D1-D3 in L4 all connect to O1 in L5 (convergence); O1 then connects to O2. Additional feedback: from O2 in L5, use a dashed arrow back to P1 in L3 (representing a feedback loop). All arrows must be orthogonal right-angle lines, cross-container connections must run only in the blank areas between containers, never passing through module interiors; modules inside containers are aligned in neat columns/rows with consistent spacing. In the bottom right corner, place a small legend box containing two example lines: solid arrow = FLOW, dashed arrow = FEEDBACK. Overall use only black/gray/blue colors, text uses only Latin letters and numerals.

White background, horizontal canvas, mathematical comprehensive four-panel vector line art. Divide the canvas into a 2×2 grid of four equal panels using thin black lines, with 4% outer margin and consistent spacing between panels; place small labels A, B, C, D (sans-serif) in the upper left corner of each panel. Panel A: Place an acute triangle ABC on a light gray circumscribed circle (A at top, B at bottom left, C at bottom right), three sides as black solid lines; draw three medians intersecting at G, draw three altitudes intersecting at H, and mark right angle symbols at the feet of the altitudes (D on BC, E on CA, F on AB). Panel B: Cartesian coordinate system with light gray grid lines, x-axis and y-axis with tick marks from -4 to 4; plot the parabola y=(x-1)^2-3 (blue curve) and the line y=2x-1 (black straight line), mark the two intersection points P1 and P2 (hollow circles), and use gray dashed lines to project P1 and P2 vertically onto the x-axis; fill the enclosed region between the two curves from P1 to P2 with light gray 45° diagonal hatching. Panel C: Complex plane/unit circle diagram, draw coordinate axes with light gray grid, draw the unit circle; from the origin draw three blue vectors to points Z1, Z2, Z3 on the circle at angles 30°, 150°, 270° respectively, annotate with arcs labeled ANG1/ANG2/ANG3 with directional arrows on the arcs; use a gray dashed line to indicate the mapping of Z1 rotated 90° to Z1I (labeled ROT90), and draw a counterclockwise directional arrow segment on the circumference. Panel D: Left half is an undirected graph: 6 nodes V1–V6 evenly placed at the vertices of a regular hexagon, draw outer ring edges V1-V2-…-V6-V1, and additionally draw three diagonal chords V1-V4, V2-V5, V3-V6; right half is a 6×6 adjacency matrix (thin-lined table), row and column headers are V1…V6, main diagonal is 0, fill 1 for connected edges and 0 for no edge according to the graph, matrix must be symmetric and exactly consistent with the left graph. Entire image has no shadows and no perspective, colors are only black/gray/blue, all text uses only Latin letters and numerals.

White background horizontal canvas, a three-column side-by-side classical mechanics comprehensive diagram (vector line art), three columns of equal width and height, separated by thin gray vertical lines, 4% outer margin. Left column: inclined plane-pulley-hanging weight system schematic. Draw an inclined plane with the angle to horizontal marked THETA=30 (using arc and arrow), a block m1 placed on the inclined plane; draw a pulley at the top of the inclined plane, a thin rope passes over the pulley connecting to a hanging block m2; draw coordinate axes x along the incline direction and y in the normal direction (with arrows), and use dashed lines to indicate the two rope segments maintaining parallel/perpendicular relationships. Middle column: Free Body Diagram (FBD) analysis, upper and lower aligned. Upper half is the FBD for m1: draw a small block, four main forces represented by blue arrows labeled N (perpendicular to incline pointing outward), T (along rope direction), F (along incline opposite to possible motion direction), mg (vertically downward); use gray dashed lines to decompose mg into components along the incline and normal direction, labeled mgx and mgy respectively. Lower half is the FBD for m2: small block, blue arrow T pointing upward, blue arrow mg pointing downward, both collinear. Right column: three time-curve small graphs stacked vertically with x-axes aligned (sharing the same t-axis scale). Top graph is a(t): a horizontal constant line; middle graph is v(t): a straight line increasing from the origin; bottom graph is x(t): an upward-opening parabola; each small graph has coordinate axes with tick marks, labeled a, v, x and t respectively; at the same moment t1, a vertical dashed line passes through all three graphs with small dots at the intersection points. The entire diagram uses only black/gray/blue colors, consistent line width, all text uses only Latin letters and numbers.

White background horizontal canvas, a combined infographic of circuit schematic + frequency response + time-domain response (vector line art), overall two-row layout: upper row occupies 60% height for circuit schematic, lower row occupies 40% height for chart area; modules framed with thin gray borders and aligned to a 12-column grid. Upper row circuit: draw a two-stage active filter/amplifier circuit including op-amps U1 and U2 (standard triangle symbols with + and - inputs marked), resistors R1-R6 (zigzag symbols), capacitors C1-C3 (parallel plate symbols), input source VIN (circular source symbol) and output VOUT (test point dot); power rails labeled +V and -V connected to U1/U2; all ground points use standard ground symbols; key nodes labeled N1-N6; all wiring must be horizontal/vertical with right-angle corners, no diagonal lines allowed; clearly draw the resistor/capacitor feedback network from output back to inverting input. Lower row chart area: left 2/3 is a Bode plot (two plots stacked vertically sharing the same logarithmic frequency axis): upper plot shows MAG(dB) vs f curve with two-corner rolloff (-40 dB/dec trend), vertical dashed lines marking f1 and f2; lower plot shows PHASE(deg) curve gradually decreasing from 0 to approximately -180, also marking f1 and f2. Right 1/3 is a step response plot: horizontal axis t, vertical axis Vout, input step Vin drawn as gray square wave reference, output as blue rising curve with slight overshoot and damped oscillation, horizontal dashed lines marking peak point P and steady-state value SS. All axes with tick marks and aligned, uniform line weights, colors only black/gray/blue, all text in Latin letters and numbers only.

White square canvas, 2×2 four-panel electromagnetism field diagram collection (vector line art), panels equal-sized, 4% outer margin, consistent inter-panel spacing, each panel labeled A/B/C/D in upper left corner. Panel A: Electric dipole field line diagram, left and right each with a small circle representing point charges, left is +Q and right is -Q (symbols clearly visible), multiple electric field lines emanating from +Q curving into -Q, field lines with directional arrows and perfectly mirror-symmetric top/bottom/left/right; additionally draw several equipotential lines (gray dashed closed curves) orthogonal to field lines. Panel B: Parallel plate capacitor electric field, draw two thick black horizontal metal plates, upper plate marked +, lower plate marked -, uniform vertical electric field lines between plates (arrows pointing top to bottom), curved fringing field lines flaring outward at both edges, and several horizontal gray dashed equipotential lines (approximately parallel to plates in between). Panel C: Straight wire magnetic field, center draw a current symbol: circle with dot inside (representing current out of page), surrounded by multiple concentric circular magnetic field lines (blue), each circle with evenly spaced directional arrows indicating counterclockwise; beside it draw another circle with cross inside (representing current into page) with corresponding clockwise concentric circles (both groups side by side in same panel, equal size). Panel D: Lorentz force diagram for charged particle in uniform magnetic field, draw a rectangular region representing uniform magnetic field, array of cross symbols evenly distributed inside indicating B pointing into page; a particle (small dot) enters from left side, velocity vector v pointing right (blue arrow), force F pointing up (blue arrow), trajectory is an upward-curving circular arc; label q, v, B, F, with F always perpendicular to v. Entire image no shadows no gradients, colors only black/gray/blue, all text only Latin letters and symbols.

White background horizontal canvas, three-panel optical ray diagram + bottom parameter table (vector line art). Upper three panels equally wide side-by-side (4% outer margin, uniform inter-panel spacing), lower section a horizontal table spanning full width. Left Panel A: Thin lens imaging diagram, draw principal optical axis as horizontal straight line, vertical lens symbol at center; symmetrically mark focal points F1, F2 and 2F1, 2F2 on both sides of lens; place a vertical object arrow OBJ (pointing upward) on left side, construct standard three principal rays: 1) parallel ray enters then passes through right focal point; 2) ray through lens center continues straight without deflection; 3) ray through left focal point exits parallel to principal axis; at intersection of three rays draw inverted image arrow IMG with dashed auxiliary lines for positioning. Middle Panel B: Concave mirror imaging diagram, draw principal axis, draw concave mirror arc on right side, mark center of curvature C and focal point F; place object arrow OBJ between F and C in front of mirror, draw two typical reflected rays yielding inverted magnified real image IMG (located beyond C), indicate reflection law with normal line and incident/reflection angle arcs labeled A1, A2. Right Panel C: Triangular prism dispersion ray path, draw equilateral triangle prism, a single white main ray beam enters from left and refracts inside prism, exit end splits into three separated rays (using red/green/blue thin colored lines) fanning out; draw normal lines at entry and exit points, mark angles I1, R1, I2, R2 with arcs; refracted ray inside prism must be straight line segment, exit color rays separate at increasing angles in order R, G, B. Bottom parameter table: 4 columns × 3 rows thin-line table, headers ITEM, SYMBOL, VALUE, UNIT; rows contain AIR n 1.00, GLASS n 1.50, LENS f 50 mm (all in Latin letters and numerals). Uniform line weight throughout, no shadows no gradients, color scheme primarily black/gray, necessary light rays in blue and RGB.

White background, horizontal canvas, biology four-panel comprehensive diagram (vector line art), divided into 2×2 four equal-sized panels with thin black lines, 4% outer margin, consistent spacing between panels, corner labels A/B/C/D. Panel A: Animal cell cross-section schematic, draw cell membrane outer contour and cytoplasm, interior contains: nucleus (double-line nuclear envelope, nucleolus as small circle), mitochondria (oval with cristae lines), rough endoplasmic reticulum (wavy tubular with dotted ribosomes), Golgi apparatus (stacked curved cisternae), lysosomes (small circles), centrosome (two short cylinders); each structure connected to edge labels via leader lines: N, NU, M, RER, G, L, CEN (Latin abbreviations only). Panel B: Neuron structure + action potential. Upper half depicts neuron: dendrites, soma, axon, segmented myelin sheath with nodes of Ranvier, terminal synaptic boutons; arrows indicate signal propagation direction. Lower half depicts action potential curve: axes t and V, curve includes resting phase, depolarization spike, repolarization, hyperpolarization recovery; draw one threshold dashed line TH with annotation. Panel C: Gene regulatory network diagram, 10 nodes G1-G10 arranged in two-layer layout (4 upper, 6 lower), activation relationships shown with arrow connections, inhibition relationships shown with T-bar flat-head connections; include at least 2 feedback loops and 1 feedforward motif, use curved lines to avoid overlap, nodes are equal-sized rounded rectangles. Panel D: Gel electrophoresis diagram, draw a rectangular gel with 10 loading wells at top; lane 1 is LADDER (multiple regularly spaced bands), lanes 2-10 are samples S1-S9, each lane has different band numbers and positions but horizontally aligned, bands are dark gray rectangles; draw molecular weight scale lines on left side of gel (100, 200, 300... using short numbers). Overall only black/gray/blue colors, no shadows no gradients, all text uses only Latin letters and numerals.

White background horizontal canvas, geographic/topographic map combination infographic (vector line art). Left 70% contains the main contour topographic map, right 30% contains three vertically stacked auxiliary panels (location inset, small cross-section, climate chart), all module frames in thin gray and aligned. Left main map: draw a contour map of a rectangular terrain area, contour interval is 20 (numerically labeled on several index contours, such as 100, 120, 140, 160, 180), contour lines in brown-gray thin strokes; draw one hill (multiple closed concentric contours) and one U-shaped valley, a blue river winds from upstream through the valley bottom to downstream and exits the boundary; draw a black road crossing the river (bridge indicated by two short parallel lines); overlay a coordinate grid on the map (columns A-D, rows 1-4 labeled on the border frame), place a north arrow N in the upper right corner, place a scale bar in the lower left corner (0-1-2 km). Draw a cross-section line A-B on the main map (thick black line, labeled A and B at both ends). Right upper panel: location inset map, draw a simplified rectangular outline with a highlighted box indicating the main map extent, and add a pointing arrow. Right middle panel: terrain profile along A-B, horizontal axis labeled DIST, vertical axis labeled ELEV, curve undulation corresponds to main map contour lines, and mark the river crossing point below the profile. Right lower panel: climate chart, horizontal axis shows months 1-12, left vertical axis TEMP with a line graph, right vertical axis PREC with 12 bar columns (dual-axis same chart), line and bars aligned. Overall color restriction: terrain lines brown-gray, river blue, all else black/gray; no shadows no gradients, all text uses only Latin letters and numerals.

White background, horizontal canvas, single complex 3D geometric figure (vector line drawing, no perspective, isometric projection). Draw a cube in isometric view (three edge groups oriented at ±30° to horizontal and vertical directions); visible edges rendered with thick black solid lines, hidden edges with thin gray dashed lines. Label the 8 vertices as A, B, C, D (top face, clockwise) and E, F, G, H (bottom face, corresponding directly below); mark vertices with small solid black dots. Edge connectivity must be strictly maintained: A-B-C-D forms the closed top face, E-F-G-H forms the closed bottom face, with vertical edges A-E, B-F, C-G, D-H. Then construct a cross-section: locate the midpoints of 6 edges and label them P1–P6 (using blue hollow circle markers); the 6 edges in sequence are AB, BC, CG, GH, HE, EA. Connect P1-P2-P3-P4-P5-P6-P1 with blue thick dashed lines to form a closed hexagonal section line; fill the hexagon interior with very light blue semi-transparent color (no gradient). Near the section, draw a blue normal vector n (arrow) perpendicular to the section polygon plane, with a thin gray leader line labeling "PLANE π". Add thin gray auxiliary lines from each Pi to both endpoints of its respective edge to emphasize the midpoint relationship. Place a small three-axis direction triad in the bottom left corner (x, y, z, black thin line arrows, Latin letters only). Entire diagram uses only black/gray/blue colors, two line weight levels (thick for contours, thin for construction), no shadows or gradients allowed, all text in Latin letters and numerals/symbols only.

White background horizontal canvas, two complex diagrams arranged side by side: P–V diagram on left, T–S diagram on right, equal width and height, separated by a thin gray vertical line; both diagrams use black coordinate axes and tick marks (values optional but tick spacing consistent). Left diagram (P–V): draw two isothermal curves (blue smooth curves resembling hyperbolas), upper one labeled TH, lower one labeled TC; draw two adiabatic curves (steeper blue curves) connecting the isothermal curves to form a closed cycle 1-2-3-4 (counterclockwise direction), with small arrows on each process indicating direction. Four state points marked with blue solid dots labeled 1,2,3,4: 1 at bottom-left, 2 at bottom-right, 3 at top-right, 4 at top-left; process 1→2 follows the lower isothermal curve, 2→3 follows the right adiabatic curve, 3→4 follows the upper isothermal curve, 4→1 follows the left adiabatic curve. Right diagram (T–S): draw a blue rectangular cycle, top horizontal line labeled TH, bottom horizontal line labeled TC, left and right sides are vertical lines (representing adiabatic isentropic processes); four corner points labeled 1,2,3,4 with numbering order identical to left diagram (1 bottom-left, 2 bottom-right, 3 top-right, 4 top-left), with directional arrows on each segment making it clockwise. Above the top horizontal segment of right diagram, add a thin gray bracket labeled QH; below the bottom horizontal segment, label QC; inside the rectangle, use thin gray annotation to mark the cycle area as "W". Overall color scheme strictly black/gray/blue only, uniform line width throughout, no shadows or gradients, all text limited to Latin letters and numerals only.

White background horizontal canvas, two complex diagrams arranged side by side: left shows a planar 2R robotic arm kinematics schematic, right shows the workspace diagram; both panels equal width, separated by a thin gray vertical line in the center. Left panel: place a base coordinate frame at origin O (x-axis pointing right, y-axis pointing up, black arrows), draw the first link from O to elbow point J with length annotation L1=3 (dimension line in light gray); draw the second link from J to end-effector P with length annotation L2=2. Select one configuration and clearly annotate: θ1=30° (angle from +x axis counterclockwise to the first link, shown with a gray arc and arrow), θ2=60° (relative angle at elbow J from the first link to the second link, shown with a gray arc). Joints O and J as black solid circles, end-effector P as a blue solid circle labeled P. Draw thin gray dashed lines projecting P onto the x-axis and y-axis, with approximate coordinates written nearby as "x≈2.60, y≈3.50" (Latin letters and numerals only). Right panel: draw x–y coordinate axes (black), draw two concentric circles centered at origin O: inner circle radius rmin=1, outer circle radius rmax=5 (thin black lines), fill the annular region between the two circles with very light gray (no gradient), labeled "REACHABLE". Mark point P corresponding to the same end-effector position as in the left panel (blue solid dot) within this annular region, its position should be roughly consistent with radius 4.36 (within r=5 and clearly greater than r=1), with a thin gray leader line labeling "P". Also draw a blue radius line from the origin to P and label it r. Color scheme: only black/gray/blue, uniform line weights, no shadows no gradients, all text in Latin letters and numerals/symbols only.

White background horizontal canvas, two complex image panels combined: left shows FCC crystal structure with (111) plane, right shows 2D close-packed projection on the (111) plane; both panels equal width and height, separated by a thin gray vertical line in the middle. Left panel: draw an isometric view of a cubic unit cell (black thin line framework), label crystal axes a, b, c (small three-axis arrows, Latin). Draw atoms at the 8 corner points (gray small spheres/circles), draw atoms at the 6 face centers (gray larger spheres/circles, positioned at the center of each face). Use a very light blue semi-transparent polygon to represent the (111) plane: this plane forms a triangular cross-section within the unit cell, with three vertices located at the coordinate intercept points (a,0,0), (0,a,0), (0,0,a) on the corresponding three edge endpoints; outline this triangular face with blue stroke, and write "(111)" on the face. Draw a blue arrow in the plane normal direction and label it "[111]". Also mark three face-center atom positions within the plane (located at (a/2,a/2,0), (a/2,0,a/2), (0,a/2,a/2)) and label them F1, F2, F3 with thin gray leader lines (no coordinates needed). Right panel: draw the 2D projection triangular lattice of the (111) plane: use gray circles to represent atoms, arranged in hexagonal close-packed pattern (at least 3 rows, at least 5 atoms per row, staggered between rows); draw two lattice vectors a1 and a2 (black arrows) at 60° angle, and mark "60°" with a small arc. Outline a minimum parallelogram primitive cell in blue stroke within the lattice, and label it "2D CELL". Use thin gray dashed lines to mark 3 specific atoms in the right panel as F1, F2, F3 (same labels as left panel) to emphasize correspondence. Color scheme only black/gray/blue, no shadows or gradients allowed, all text in Latin letters, numbers, and symbols only.

White background, horizontal canvas, single complex analytic geometry comprehensive diagram (vector line art, all geometric relationships must be self-consistent). Draw a Cartesian coordinate system with light gray square grid (x-axis horizontal pointing right, y-axis vertical pointing up), display range x∈[-3,13], y∈[-6,6], with tick marks every 1 unit as small grid and every 2 units as thick grid. Construct two intersecting circles: C1: x^2 + y^2 = 9 (center O1(0,0), radius 3), C2: (x-4)^2 + y^2 = 4 (center O2(4,0), radius 2). Circles drawn with black thin solid lines. Mark O1 and O2 (black solid dots + labels), and connect O1O2 with a thin gray line segment. Mark the two intersection points X and Y, positions must precisely satisfy: X(21/8, 3√15/8), Y(21/8, -3√15/8) (blue solid dots), draw the common chord XY with blue thick line, and extend this line to show the RADICAL AXIS (the same vertical line x=21/8). On the x-axis mark the external center of similitude S(12,0) (red hollow circle dot + label S), from S draw two external common tangent lines t+ and t- (red thick solid lines), which must be tangent to both C1 and C2 and located above and below respectively. Mark the tangent points: T1, T2 on C1, and U1, U2 on C2; draw radii O1T1, O1T2, O2U1, O2U2 (thin gray), and at each tangent point draw a right angle symbol indicating radius ⊥ tangent line. To avoid ambiguity, place a small table in the upper right corner (light gray border, no fill, 3 rows × 3 columns), header row: ITEM / EXACT / APPROX; row 1: S, (12,0), (12.000,0.000); row 2: X, (21/8, 3√15/8), (2.625, 1.452); row 3: T on C1, (3/4, ±3√15/4), (0.750, ±2.905). Note: the ± symbol appears only once to represent the two vertically symmetric points. Entire diagram uses only black/gray/blue/red colors, no gradients or shadows allowed; all text in the diagram uses only Latin letters, numerals, and symbols.

White background horizontal canvas, single complex "Function Properties Analysis Infographic" (vector style). Two-column layout: left column approximately 65% width for main function graph, right column approximately 35% divided into upper and lower blocks (upper: derivative graph; lower: complex symbol table). Left column: On a coordinate system with light gray grid, plot function f(x) = (x^2 + 1)/(x^2 - 1), x range [-4,4], y range [-6,6]; function curve in blue thick line, must draw three separate branches with discontinuities at x=±1. Draw two vertical asymptotes x=-1 and x=1 as red thin dashed lines, labeled "x=-1" and "x=1" at their tops; draw horizontal asymptote y=1 as gray thin dashed line labeled "y=1". Mark and label points (blue filled dots + labels) A(0,-1), B(2,5/3), C(-2,5/3). Near the four asymptotic sides, add very small gray annotations for limit behavior: as x→-1⁻ f→+∞, as x→-1⁺ f→-∞, as x→1⁻ f→-∞, as x→1⁺ f→+∞ (small arrow notations close to curve ends). Right upper block: Plot derivative function f'(x) = -4x/(x^2 - 1)^2 (same x range [-4,4], y range [-6,6]), curve in purple medium-thick line; similarly show discontinuities at x=±1 with vertical asymptotes (reuse red thin dashed lines), and draw horizontal reference line y=0 (black thin line). Mark derivative zero point D(0,0) at x=0. Right lower block: Draw a "Sign and Properties Table" (light gray border, no fill, bold header), columns for four intervals: (-∞,-1), (-1,0), (0,1), (1,∞); rows include at minimum: SIGN f(x), SIGN f'(x), SIGN f''(x), MONOTONE, CONCAVITY. Fill using symbols "+ / - / 0 / ↑ / ↓ / ∪ / ∩": f'(x) sign must show "+" for x<0, "-" for x>0; f''(x) based on f''(x)=4(3x^2+1)/(x^2-1)^3, requiring "+" (∪) for |x|>1, "-" (∩) for |x|<1. Entire image uses only black/gray/red/blue/purple five colors, no gradient shadows; all text in the image consists only of Latin letters, numerals, and mathematical symbols.

White background, horizontal canvas, single complex "poster-style table" (vector style, emphasis on complex table layout). Title placed at top center: CONIC SECTIONS QUICK TABLE (black bold). Below, a large table occupying 80% of the canvas: requires two-layer header (with merged cells), light gray outer frame for the entire table, thinner and lighter internal grid lines; header background in very light gray, content area rows with alternating light gray shading (zebra stripes). Header Row 1: CONIC (spans 2 rows), EQUATIONS (spans 3 columns), GEOMETRY (spans 2 columns), NOTES (spans 2 rows). Header Row 2 subdivides under EQUATIONS: STANDARD, GENERAL, PARAMETRIC; under GEOMETRY: FOCUS/DIRECTRIX, ECCENTRICITY e. Content rows at least 4: CIRCLE, ELLIPSE, PARABOLA, HYPERBOLA. Each row has a small line-art icon (black lines only) on the left side of the CONIC column: circle, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola (each with small coordinate axes). STANDARD column shows typical center-form equations (e.g., circle: x²+y²=r²; ellipse: x²/a²+y²/b²=1; parabola: y²=4ax; hyperbola: x²/a²-y²/b²=1). GENERAL column shows general quadratic form Ax²+Bxy+Cy²+Dx+Ey+F=0 with small annotations in corresponding rows indicating B=0 or B≠0 conditions (Latin only). PARAMETRIC column shows parametric equations (e.g., circle: x=r cos t, y=r sin t, etc.). FOCUS/DIRECTRIX column lists focus and directrix forms in short entries; ECCENTRICITY column shows corresponding values e=0, 0<e<1, e=1, e>1. NOTES column must include at least three types of information symbols: • bullet, → arrow, ≈ approximate. Bottom right corner has a footnote row (extremely small gray text): "All formulas assume Cartesian coordinates; a,b,r>0." Color palette only black/gray/blue (blue used only for emphasis on header underlines or key symbols), no gradients or shadows; all text only Latin letters, numbers, and mathematical symbols.

White background horizontal canvas, generate a "single-page PPT style" structured infographic (vector flat style, strictly grid-aligned). Safe margins around all edges, thin black title bar at top: left side reads GEOMETRY & FUNCTIONS, right side reads SLIDE 01 (small gray text). Layout consists of three blocks: left vertical column (~30% width) is an OUTLINE bullet list (at least 5 items, using •), content Latin only: Vertex form, Axis of symmetry, Focus/directrix, Transformations, Key points table. Upper right area (~45% height) shows comparison of three quadratic function curves on the same coordinate system: y1=x^2 (black), y2=(x-2)^2+1 (blue), y3=0.5(x+1)^2-2 (red); coordinate system with light gray grid and tick marks, range x∈[-5,5], y∈[-4,6]; small legend box in upper right corner labeling y1, y2, y3. Lower right area (~55% height) split into two smaller blocks: lower-right-left block is an analytic geometry illustration: for y2=(x-2)^2+1 draw its axis of symmetry x=2 (gray dashed line), vertex V(2,1) (blue dot), focus F(2,1.5) (blue hollow dot) and directrix y=0.5 (gray dashed line), with right angle symbols/equidistant markers emphasizing "distance to focus = distance to directrix". Lower-right-right block is a complex small table (at least 4 columns × 4 rows, light gray borders, header with light gray background): columns are FUNC, a, h, k; rows list y1, y2, y3 and one blank row for "custom". All text Latin letters, numbers and symbols only; colors limited to black/gray/blue/red; no gradients or shadows, overall presenting professional PPT layout.

White background horizontal canvas, two complex image compositions: left is a 3D analytical geometry scene, right contains two orthogonal projections plus a data table (vector line art, semi-transparency allowed but gradients prohibited). Left panel (approximately 60% width): Draw a 3D coordinate system x, y, z (black thin arrow lines), draw a cylindrical surface x² + y² = 4 (extending along z direction, rendered as a transparent cylinder using gray wireframe/latitude-longitude grid mesh), then draw a plane z = x + 1 (represented as a light blue semi-transparent quadrilateral labeled "PLANE"). Highlight the intersection curve of both surfaces with a thick red line (closed space curve). Mark four parameter points on the intersection curve (red solid dots + labels): t=0 point P0(2,0,3), t=π/2 point P1(0,2,1), t=π point P2(-2,0,-1), t=3π/2 point P3(0,-2,1). Right panel (approximately 40% width) divided into top, middle, and bottom sections: Top section is x–y projection: draw circle x²+y²=4 (black thin line) and mark projected points P0–P3; Middle section is y–z projection: draw circle y² + (z-1)² = 4 (black thin line) and mark the same four projected points (note center at (0,1)); Bottom section is a table (light gray border, 5 columns × 5 rows): columns are t, x, y, z, note, rows contain coordinates for t=0, π/2, π, 3π/2, with the note column showing "MAX z", "z=1", "MIN z", "z=1" respectively. At the top of the right panel, add a small formula box with parametric equations: x=2 cos t, y=2 sin t, z=2 cos t + 1. Color scheme limited to black/gray/light blue/red only, no shadows or gradients; all text in the image uses only Latin letters, numbers, and symbols (π allowed).

Horizontal canvas, HD vector information dashboard (flat design, no 3D or perspective tilting allowed), theme: SaaS KPI Dashboard — 2025. Overall layout uses a 12-column grid with ample margins, light gray background + white card sections, subtle shadows only allowed to be very slight (optional). Top row contains 4 KPI cards (large numbers + small descriptions + period-over-period arrow icons): TOTAL REVENUE = USD 1.991M; TOTAL NEW CUSTOMERS = 5,620; AVG CHURN = 3.48%; ENDING MAU = 74k. Main area left side features a dual Y-axis line chart: title Revenue & MAU (Monthly), x-axis Jan–Dec, left y-axis Revenue (USD k) range 100–240, right y-axis MAU (k) range 40–80; Revenue shown as cyan-blue thick line with circular point markers, MAU shown as purple thick line with square point markers; annotate "Dec peak" at the Dec point. Main area right top features a small line chart: Churn Rate (%), x-axis Jan–Dec, y-axis range 2.5–4.5; using orange-red line with data labels at Jan=4.2 and Dec=2.9. Main area right middle features a stacked bar chart: New Customers by Channel (Quarterly), 4 bars Q1–Q4, each bar displays total at top; stacked segments and data must be precise: Q1 total 1020 = Organic 380 + Paid Search 260 + Referrals 220 + Partners 160; Q2 total 1290 = 470 + 340 + 270 + 210; Q3 total 1500 = 560 + 390 + 320 + 230; Q4 total 1810 = 650 + 480 + 390 + 290. Main area right bottom features a donut chart: Dec Revenue Split (displaying percentage and value in USD k), divided into 4 segments totaling 220: Core 121 (55%), Add-ons 55 (25%), Services 26 (12%), Other 18 (8%); annotated with leader lines on the outside. Bottom horizontal area contains a data table (12 rows × 5 columns, light gray grid lines, header bolded): MONTH / REVENUE_k / MAU_k / CHURN_% / NEW_CUSTOMERS, monthly data must be precise: Jan 120,48,4.2,320; Feb 128,50,4.0,340; Mar 135,52,3.8,360; Apr 150,55,3.6,410; May 160,57,3.5,450; Jun 158,56,3.7,430; Jul 170,60,3.4,480; Aug 180,63,3.3,520; Sep 175,62,3.2,500; Oct 190,66,3.1,560; Nov 205,70,3.0,600; Dec 220,74,2.9,650. Color scheme requirements: rich but unified: Revenue #00A6D6, MAU #7C3AED, Churn #F97316, Organic #22C55E, Paid Search #3B82F6, Referrals #F59E0B, Partners #EC4899, auxiliary gray #94A3B8; all text in English and numbers.

Horizontal canvas, two complex images arranged side by side (thin gray dividing line in the middle), vector style, theme: Energy Mix & Emissions (2015–2025). Left panel: 100% stacked area chart, title "Generation Mix (%)", x-axis years 2015 to 2025 with one tick per year, y-axis 0–100%; 6 energy layers from bottom to top in fixed order with consistent colors: Coal (dark gray #334155), Gas (orange #F97316), Nuclear (purple #8B5CF6), Hydro (blue #0EA5E9), Wind (teal #10B981), Solar (yellow #FACC15). Yearly percentage data must be precise with each year summing to 100: 2015 38/27/19/7/7/2; 2016 36/28/19/7/8/2; 2017 34/28/19/7/9/3; 2018 32/29/19/7/10/3; 2019 30/29/19/7/11/4; 2020 28/29/19/7/12/5; 2021 26/29/19/7/13/6; 2022 24/28/19/7/14/8; 2023 22/27/19/7/16/9; 2024 20/26/19/7/18/10; 2025 18/25/19/7/20/11 (order: Coal/Gas/Nuclear/Hydro/Wind/Solar). Draw vertical dashed lines at 2018, 2021, 2024 with small annotation labels: 2018 "Carbon tax"; 2021 "Wind expansion"; 2024 "Solar subsidy". Right panel upper section: donut chart, title "Mix in 2025", displaying 6 category percentages (consistent with left panel 2025 data), with "2025" written in the center of the donut. Right panel lower section: line chart, title "CO2 Intensity (gCO2/kWh)", x-axis 2015–2025, y-axis range 350–540; red #EF4444 thick line with circle markers, data must be precise: 2015 520, 2016 505, 2017 490, 2018 470, 2019 455, 2020 440, 2021 425, 2022 410, 2023 395, 2024 380, 2025 370; add annotation "370" at the 2025 point. Overall bottom: a compact data table (3 rows × 12 columns), row names are "Coal%", "Solar%", "CO2 intensity", column names are 2015–2025, filled with corresponding data from above for verification; all text in English and numbers, rich but not glaring colors, no 3D effects or heavy shadows.

A4 landscape poster-style infographic, vector flat style, theme: BUDGET BREAKDOWN 2026 (fictional). Place a dual-layer nested donut chart in the center: inner ring with 3 main categories, outer ring with 12 subcategories; all values and percentages must be strictly consistent. Total budget TOTAL = USD 2.40B written below the title. Inner ring with amounts: Operations USD 1.08B (45%), Capital Projects USD 0.84B (35%), Community Programs USD 0.48B (20%). Outer ring subdivisions using same color family shades as inner ring (allow tonal variations within same color family but no realistic gradient lighting): Operations subdivides into Personnel 0.52B, Maintenance 0.28B, IT & Security 0.18B, Other Ops 0.10B; Capital Projects subdivides into Transit 0.30B, Housing 0.22B, Schools 0.18B, Water & Roads 0.14B; Community Programs subdivides into Health 0.16B, Education Grants 0.12B, Parks 0.10B, Arts 0.10B. Each outer ring sector must have leader lines labeled with "Name + USD + % of total", ensuring percentages calculated based on 2.40B. Place a vertical bar chart on the right side: YoY Change by Subcategory (%), x-axis shows 12 subcategories (abbreviated labels), y-axis from -4 to +10; bars use dual colors for positive/negative (positive growth in green #22C55E, negative growth in red #EF4444), data must be precise: Personnel +3, Maintenance +1, IT & Security +6, Other Ops -2, Transit +4, Housing +5, Schools +2, Water & Roads -1, Health +7, Education Grants +3, Parks 0, Arts +1; zero line must be bold. Place a reconciliation table at the bottom (4 columns × 13 rows): CATEGORY / SUBCATEGORY / USD (B) / YoY%, list all 12 subcategories row by row with their parent categories and corresponding data, and write TOTAL USD 2.40B at the bottom row. Rich color requirements: suggested main colors for three categories are blue #3B82F6, purple #8B5CF6, orange #F59E0B, subcategories use different shades within same color family; aligned layout, sufficient whitespace, no 3D. All text in English and numbers.

Online flight booking and seat selection webpage (realistic e-commerce/travel site UI screenshot aesthetic). Top section features a step progress bar (Search - Details - Seat Selection - Payment - Confirmation). Main body uses a left-right column layout: left side contains a complex form area (passenger information block cards, collapsible sections, with multi-line input fields and dropdown placeholders), right side displays a price and itinerary summary card (including a small pie chart: fare breakdown composition). Below the form area, three structural modules must appear simultaneously: 1) Seat map: airplane seat grid (divided by cabin class zones, with row and column numbers, some seats unavailable shown as gray blocks); 2) Price calendar: monthly view grid (7 columns with multiple rows, cells containing price placeholders and lowest-price markers); 3) Cabin class comparison table: multi-column comparison table (different cabin classes as columns, rows including baggage/rebooking/refund items, with checkmark/cross icon placeholders). At the bottom of the right summary area, include a timeline component (departure-layover-arrival nodes with connecting lines). Requires multiple complex grids/tables/timeline structures combined while maintaining proper webpage alignment.

Horizontal canvas, city mobility data dashboard (vector flat style, colorful, clear legends), theme: City Mobility — Monthly Ridership & Revenue. Top section with 3 KPI cards: Total trips = 111.4M; Peak month = Aug (10.45M); Mode share in Aug = Bus 3.9 / Metro 5.3 / Bike 1.25 (displayed in small text). Left main chart is a multi-line chart: Monthly Trips (M), x-axis Jan–Dec, y-axis 0–6; three lines with fixed colors: Bus (green #22C55E), Metro (blue #3B82F6), Bike-share (orange #F97316), all with point markers. Data must be exact: Bus=[3.2,3.1,3.3,3.5,3.6,3.7,3.8,3.9,3.7,3.6,3.4,3.3]; Metro=[4.5,4.4,4.6,4.8,5.0,5.1,5.2,5.3,5.1,5.0,4.8,4.7]; Bike=[0.6,0.5,0.7,0.9,1.1,1.2,1.3,1.25,1.0,0.9,0.7,0.65]. Add annotation "Heatwave" at Jul, add annotation "Storm" at Sep. Upper right is a stacked bar chart: Fare Revenue by Payment Type (USD M), x-axis Q1–Q4, y-axis 0–170; 4 stacked segments with rich colors and clear legend: Contactless, Card, Cash, Pass; data must be exact: Q1 45/30/15/30=120; Q2 55/32/13/35=135; Q3 65/34/11/40=150; Q4 60/33/12/35=140 (order Contactless/Card/Cash/Pass), show totals at top of bars. Lower right is a pie chart: Rider Profile Share, must be exact: Commuter 52%, Student 18%, Senior 12%, Tourist 10%, Other 8%, label percentage outside each slice with legend. Bottom section contains a small table (4 columns × 4 rows): Mode / 2025 Total (M) / Avg per month / Peak month, Mode rows Bus=42.1, Metro=58.5, Bike=10.8 (sum 111.4), calculate Avg (monthly average) to 1 decimal place; Peak month for Bus and Metro is Aug, for Bike determine based on data. No 3D effects or heavy shadows; all text in English numerals.

Screenshot of a no-code automation workflow editor web UI (similar to a flow builder). Top section features a breadcrumb navigation plus save/publish button bar; left side contains a component/trigger palette (categorized collapsible sections with draggable items); center displays an infinite canvas containing a complex flowchart (start node, multiple action nodes, conditional diamond branches, parallel branches, loop circuits, error handling branches; nodes connected by arrowed connectors with different styles: solid/dashed lines; mini-map thumbnail in the bottom-right corner of the canvas). Right side shows a properties panel (accordion collapsible sections: input/output/retry strategy/log level, containing toggles, dropdowns, and text field placeholders). Bottom section features an expandable run log table (columns: time, step, status, duration, details button), with a horizontal step progress bar above the table (numbered dots from 1 to N). The overall composition must present a realistic webpage layout with high-density structure.

A complete architectural floor plan of a residential apartment featuring a 3-bedroom, 2-living area, 2-bathroom layout, with all room dimensions labeled in millimeters, including a living room, dining room, master bedroom (with en-suite bathroom and walk-in closet), secondary bedroom, study, kitchen, and balcony, using dashed lines to indicate the swing direction of sliding doors and hinged doors, load-bearing walls marked with thick solid lines and partition walls marked with thin solid lines, with a scale bar and north arrow drawn in the bottom right corner

A standard solution diagram for a high school physics force analysis problem: a block of mass m rests on a rough inclined plane at a 30° angle, connected by an inextensible light string passing over a fixed pulley at the top of the incline to a suspended object of mass M. Complete free-body diagrams must be drawn separately for both the block and the suspended object, labeling gravitational force mg, normal force N, frictional force f, and string tension T. Adjacent to the diagrams, establish a rectangular coordinate system with axes along the incline direction and perpendicular to the incline, showing orthogonal decomposition of all forces with component forces indicated by dashed lines

A sales data infographic containing three chart types: at the top is a pie chart divided into 6 sectors showing revenue share by product line (Electronics 35%, Clothing 22%, Food 18%, Home Goods 12%, Beauty 8%, Other 5%) distinguished by different colors; in the middle is a combination chart with dual Y-axes featuring a bar chart on the left Y-axis displaying annual sales from 2020-2025 (in billions of dollars), and a line chart on the right Y-axis showing profit margin trends over the same period; at the bottom is a 5-row by 6-column data table summarizing all the above values, with a dark shaded header row

A complex organic chemistry reaction mechanism diagram illustrating the complete pathway of the aldol condensation reaction between benzaldehyde and acetone catalyzed by NaOH: including the α-hydrogen deprotonation of acetone to generate an enolate anion, nucleophilic addition to the carbonyl carbon of benzaldehyde, protonation to form the β-hydroxy ketone intermediate, and finally dehydration to yield the α,β-unsaturated ketone product, with curved arrows indicating electron transfer direction at each step, labeling all intermediate structural formulas and reaction conditions for each stage

A three-view orthographic projection (front view, side view, top view) of a complex 3D geometric solid: the solid is a regular hexagonal prism with one corner truncated by an oblique plane. The three views are arranged according to first-angle projection method (ISO mechanical drawing standard), with the front view positioned at the upper left, the side view at the upper right, and the top view at the lower left. The views are connected by projection alignment lines (dash-dot center lines), key dimensions are annotated, and hidden edges of the cross-section are shown with dashed lines. Scale 1:2 is marked in the lower right corner.

A complete diagram of a high school analytic geometry problem: draw an ellipse x²/16+y²/9=1 in a Cartesian coordinate system, labeling the major axis endpoints A₁ and A₂, the minor axis endpoints B₁ and B₂, and the two foci F₁ and F₂; through the right focus F₂, draw a line with slope 1 intersecting the ellipse at points P and Q, connecting PQ with a solid line; from the origin O, draw a perpendicular to line PQ intersecting the ellipse at point R, marking the foot of perpendicular H and the right angle symbol with a dashed line; label on the diagram the coordinates of all key points, the length of segment PQ, and the area formula for triangle PQR

A complex analog circuit schematic: a two-stage common-emitter amplifier circuit containing two NPN transistors Q1 and Q2, each stage includes base bias resistors, collector load resistors, and emitter resistors (with bypass capacitors), the two stages are connected through a coupling capacitor, the input has a signal source (sine wave symbol) and input coupling capacitor, the output has a load resistor RL, the top of the circuit is labeled with +12V power supply and ground symbols, all components are labeled with standard symbol designations (R1-R8, C1-C4, Q1, Q2) and parameter values

A complete biological diagram of the human blood circulatory system: displaying both systemic circulation and pulmonary circulation pathways, with red color marking the oxygenated blood flowing through the arterial pathway (left ventricle → aorta → branching arteries → capillary network), with blue color marking the deoxygenated blood flowing through the venous pathway (capillary network → branching veins → superior and inferior vena cava → right atrium), pulmonary circulation marked with opposite colors (right ventricle → pulmonary artery → lung capillaries → pulmonary veins → left atrium), the heart section must show a cross-sectional view of all four chambers with labeled atrioventricular valves and semilunar valves, with arrows and text annotations on the side explaining the gas exchange process

A top-down view of a complex 20×20 maze, maze walls drawn with thick black lines, paths as white corridors, entrance at the bottom-left corner marked with a green arrow labeled 'START', exit at the top-right corner marked with a red arrow labeled 'END', the maze interior contains three special areas: a 2×2 central plaza, a ring-shaped corridor, and a dead-end dense zone, the correct path marked with a yellow dashed line, a coordinate grid drawn around the maze perimeter with row and column numbers labeled (1-20), a legend at the bottom explaining the meaning of each symbol

A detailed interdisciplinary biochemistry scientific illustration depicting the process of photosynthesis. On the left side is a microscopic cross-section of a plant mesophyll cell, clearly showing the thylakoid membranes and stroma structures inside the chloroplast; on the right side is the complex chemical molecular structure formula of chlorophyll a. The two are connected in between by an intricate biochemical reaction flowchart containing multiple arrows, cyclic pathways, and the chemical equation showing the conversion of CO2 and H2O into C6H12O6 and O2.
